profile.foundedNote: Locksmith workshop; INSTRUCTIONS.md states 1951 — website takes precedence
profile.ownershipDetail: Böhmer family; succession from 2nd to 3rd generation in progress
profile.employeesNote: ~130 incl. 17 trainees per web research; briefing states ~100
profile.revenueConfidence: low_estimate — private GmbH, single web source, upper bound

divisions.name: Steel Fabrication
divisions.description: Contract manufacturing: laser cutting, bending, MIG/MAG-TIG welding, structural steel, assemblies

divisions.name: Equestrian Products
divisions.description: Own-brand welded steel equestrian equipment: Heuraufe (hay rack) and Paddockbox (mobile paddock enclosure)
divisions.status: nascent — target: first sales within 3 years

keyPersons.name: Matthias Lukas Böhmer
keyPersons.relevance: Strategic driver of product business launch; succession pending

strategicContext: Böhmer is a classic German Mittelstand Lohnfertiger (contract manufacturer) executing a value chain jump — from pure job-shop welding to OEM product business. The trigger is structural: as larger B2B customers verticalize, Böhmer's contract order book has been declining. The 3rd generation (Matthias Böhmer) sees underutilized capacity and domain expertise in equestrian sports as a natural fit for two own-brand products.
keyConstraintsOnTransformation: No prior experience with working capital, product inventory, or outbound sales
Transition from JIT pull (customer brings material) to push (buy-stock-produce-sell) model
Capacity must serve both existing contract business and new product ramp simultaneously
